# Museum Metorite Storytelling

# Story

## Basic Knowledge

### What is a meteorite?
- If a meteoroid/asteroid/comet enters Earth’s atmosphere → it becomes a meteor (shooting star / bright streak)
- If part of it reaches the ground → it’s a meteorite

### What kind of meteorite are there? (Compositions)

| Main Class                 | Subtype / Abbreviation                       | Composition / Notes                                                                                       |
|:---------------------------|:---------------------------------------------|:----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Chondrites (Stony)**     | H                                           | Ordinary chondrite; high iron (~25–31%), many metallic grains, relatively dense                           |
|                             | L                                           | Ordinary chondrite; low iron (~20–25%), fewer metal grains, more oxidized                                   |
|                             | LL                                          | Ordinary chondrite; low iron (~19–22%), very low metal, highly oxidized                                     |
|                             | CI                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; very primitive, almost no chondrules, rich in water and organics                    |
|                             | CM                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; contains chondrules, hydrated minerals, organics                                     |
|                             | CO                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; small chondrules, primitive silicates                                               |
|                             | CV                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; large chondrules, refractory inclusions, relatively oxidized                       |
|                             | CR                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; rich in metal and chondrules, some water-bearing minerals                            |
|                             | CK                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; oxidized, high-temperature, few chondrules                                         |
|                             | CH                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; very metal-rich, tiny chondrules                                                   |
|                             | CB                                          | Carbonaceous chondrite; metal-rich, unusual, likely from planetesimal impacts                               |
| **Achondrites (Stony)**    | Eucrite, Diogenite, Aubrite, Angrite, Ureilite | Igneous/recrystallized rocks; originate from differentiated bodies (asteroids)                              |
| **Primitive Achondrites**   | Acapulcoites, Lodranites, Winonaites       | Transitional between chondrites and achondrites; partially melted and recrystallized                        |
| **Iron Meteorites**         | IAB                                         | Low–medium Ni (~6–13%), silicate inclusions, heterogeneous; from parent asteroid cores                     |
|                             | IIAB                                        | Very low Ni (~5–6%), fine octahedrite structure, mostly pure iron-nickel                                     |
|                             | IIIAB                                       | Medium Ni (~8–10%), coarse octahedrite, from large asteroid cores                                           |
|                             | IVA                                         | Ni ~8–13%, fine–medium octahedrite, low trace elements, from outer cores                                     |
|                             | IVB                                         | Ni ~16–18%, high Ni, very rare, likely from deep inner cores                                                |
| **Stony-Iron Meteorites**   | Pallasites                                  | Mixture of olivine crystals + iron-nickel; boundary between asteroid core and mantle                        |
|                             | Mesosiderites                               | Brecciated mix of silicates + iron-nickel; often from collisions of asteroids                                |
| **Enstatite Chondrites**    | EH                                          | High iron, reduced environment, unusual minerals                                                            |
|                             | EL                                          | Low iron, reduced environment, unusual minerals                                                             |
| **Planetary Meteorites**    | Lunar                                       | Ejected from the Moon by impacts; provide samples of lunar crust                                           |
|                             | Martian                                     | Ejected from Mars by impacts; provide samples of Martian crust and rocks                                     |
| **Unclassified / Other**    | Unknown, Fusion crust, Relict, Stone-ung    | Not clearly classified or only surface fragments                                                          |

## Time line

### First recorded meteorite ca. 860

- Nogata
860, 472g, Fell
Card on Timeline
- Name
- Mass
- Composition
- Location
- Image of Meteroite (Wiki)
- Wiki (Link)

### Biggest 3 meteorites

Card on Timeline
- Name
- Mass
- Composition
- Location
- Image of Meteroite (Wiki)
- Wiki (Link)

1. Hoba
1920, 60t | Found
Wheigth in London Double Decker buses (12t)
60t / 12t = 5 buses

2. Cape York | Found
1818, 58.2t

3. Campo del Cielo | Found
1575, 50t

### Meteorite hit House and Person

1954, 5.56 kg | Fell

### Mars Meteorite 
- Allan Hills 84001
1984, 1.9309 kg | Found

### Chelyabinsk Exploding meteor
2013, Fell, 100 kg


### Peak of distribution in year (year with most metorites recorded)

2003 Peak, 3323 sightings

## Other
### Distribution of found and fallen
### Distribution of meteorite over years
